mission.title: cilium: Port ranges in network policies
mission.description: Port ranges in network policies. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current cilium deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cilium version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
helm list -n cilium 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cilium installation.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
The new `endPort` field for K8s' NetworkPolicies is in [alpha in v1.21](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/network-policies/#targeting-a-range-of-ports) and likely to become stable soon.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Port ranges in network policies

```yaml
yay thanks !
@aanm 👋 
This subject came into sig-network meeting, and we need one more CNI to graduate this. I've asked to move this to GA only in v1.24, wanted to know if this is going to be viable from Cilium side.

BTW sorry about not being helpful and opening a PR to this, but I have some lack of eBPF and C knowledge!

Thanks
I think that the simplest implementation of this is "just" a matter of doing some processing in the policy internals to process the policy with a port range to generate multiple BPF map entries, one for each port. This shouldn't necessarily require BPF datapath 
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Port ranges in network policies" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
- [X] For first time contributors, read [Submitting a pull request](https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/contributing/development/contributing_guide/#submitting-a-pull-request)
- [X] All code is covered by unit and/or runtime tests where feasible.
codeSnippets:
yay thanks !
@aanm 👋 
This subject came into sig-network meeting, and we need one more CNI to graduate this. I've asked to move this to GA only in v1.24, wanted to know if this is going to be viable from Cilium side.

BTW sorry about not being helpful and opening a PR to this, but I have some lack of eBPF and C knowledge!

Thanks
I think that the simplest implementation of this is "just" a matter of doing some processing in the policy internals to process the policy with a port range to generate multiple BPF map entries, one for each port. This shouldn't necessarily require BPF datapath changes. The only danger is that if the user configures many of these, they could run into BPF map size limits though. These are also configurable so they don't have to be a hard limit, but the user s

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
